input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
git add frontend/src/hooks/useAsyncAction.ts \
  frontend/src/components/suites/ImportSuiteDrawer.tsx frontend/src/components/suites/SuiteForm.tsx \
  frontend/src/components/connections/ConnectionForm.tsx frontend/src/components/connections/ReauthModal.tsx \
  frontend/src/pages/CheckEdit.tsx frontend/src/pages/CheckNew.tsx
git commit -q -m "refactor(frontend): useAsyncAction toast hook (#199)

Extract useAsyncAction(errorPrefix) -> { run, loading }, collapsing the
\`setSubmitting(true) → try/await/success → catch message.error → finally
setSubmitting(false)\` scaffold that recurred across the six mutating forms
(connection/suite/check save, suite import, re-auth). The hook owns the loading
flag + failure toast; each caller keeps its dynamic, result-dependent success
toast inline. Matches the existing swallow-after-toast behaviour (no re-throw).

Refs #199
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>"
echo "committed #199"; git log --oneline -2 | head -2
git status -s | grep "^.M\|^??" | head
